ucs.title: [LLM] GALLIUM (Operation Soft Cell) China Chopper webshell — w3wp.exe spawning cmd/PowerShell recon on telco IIS/Exchange servers
ucs.description: Hunts the canonical GALLIUM (a.k.a. Granite Typhoon, Operation Soft Cell) initial-access-to-recon chain: a JScript/.aspx web shell (China Chopper) on an internet-facing IIS or Exchange server causes the IIS worker process w3wp.exe to spawn an interactive shell or built-in recon utility. GALLIUM ran this exact pattern at scale across telecom providers in Cybereason's Soft Cell reporting and Microsoft's Granite Typhoon write-ups — exploit of public-facing app (T1190) → web shell (T1505.003) → command/scripting interpreter (T1059.001/003) → discovery (T1018, T1049, T1033).
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdlines values(Processes.process_name) as child_procs values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="w3wp.exe" Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","powershell_ise.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","netstat.exe","tasklist.exe","quser.exe","nltest.exe","ping.exe","arp.exe","route.exe","hostname.exe","reg.exe","sc.exe","wmic.exe","schtasks.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe") by host Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.user Processes.process_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` | sort - firstTime
ucs.kql:
// GALLIUM / Granite Typhoon — China Chopper-style webshell on IIS spawning shells / recon utilities
let RecentDays = 7d;
let ReconChildren = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","powershell_ise.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","netstat.exe","tasklist.exe","quser.exe","nltest.exe","ping.exe","arp.exe","route.exe","hostname.exe","reg.exe","sc.exe","wmic.exe","schtasks.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(RecentDays)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"             // IIS / Exchange worker
| where FileName in~ (ReconChildren)
| extend AppPool = extract(@"-ap\s+\"?([^\"\s]+)\"?", 1, InitiatingProcessCommandLine)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, AccountDomain,
          AppPool,
          ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          ParentCmd   = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ChildImage  = FolderPath,
          ChildCmd    = ProcessCommandLine,
          ChildSHA256 = SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// GALLIUM / Granite Typhoon — China Chopper-style webshell on IIS spawning shells / recon utilities
let RecentDays = 7d;
let ReconChildSuffix = dynamic(["\\cmd.exe","\\powershell.exe","\\pwsh.exe","\\powershell_ise.exe","\\net.exe","\\net1.exe","\\whoami.exe","\\ipconfig.exe","\\systeminfo.exe","\\netstat.exe","\\tasklist.exe","\\quser.exe","\\nltest.exe","\\ping.exe","\\arp.exe","\\route.exe","\\hostname.exe","\\reg.exe","\\sc.exe","\\wmic.exe","\\schtasks.exe","\\bitsadmin.exe","\\certutil.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(RecentDays)
| where EventID == 4688
| where ParentProcessName endswith "\\w3wp.exe"
| where NewProcessName has_any (ReconChildSuffix)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, SubjectUserName,
          ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: GALLIUM China Chopper Webshell Spawning Recon Tool from IIS w3wp
id: e9b1f7c2-8a3d-4d5b-bf9b-9cf1d3e02a11
status: experimental
description: Detects IIS/Exchange worker process w3wp.exe spawning interactive shells or built-in discovery utilities, the canonical post-webshell pattern observed in GALLIUM / Operation Soft Cell intrusions against telecom providers.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0093/
  - https://www.cybereason.com/blog/operation-soft-cell-a-worldwide-campaign-against-telecommunications-providers
  - https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2023/11/16/granite-typhoon
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1059.003
  - attack.discovery
  - attack.g0093
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\w3wp.exe'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\net.exe'
      - '\net1.exe'
      - '\whoami.exe'
      - '\ipconfig.exe'
      - '\systeminfo.exe'
      - '\netstat.exe'
      - '\tasklist.exe'
      - '\quser.exe'
      - '\nltest.exe'
      - '\reg.exe'
      - '\wmic.exe'
      - '\schtasks.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
  condition: selection_parent and selection_child
falsepositives:
  - Genuine admin scripts launched from custom IIS management apps
  - Vulnerability scanners impersonating IIS app pools
level: high
ucs.rationale: w3wp.exe legitimately runs managed code in app-pool isolation; it almost never spawns cmd/powershell or LOLBin recon tools. China Chopper and the .aspx droppers GALLIUM left on telco Exchange/IIS servers in Soft Cell unconditionally generate this exact parent->child shape. Other Chinese clusters share webshell tradecraft (APT41, HAFNIUM) but GALLIUM's targeting (telcos with internet-facing IIS) an

ucs.title: [LLM] GALLIUM credential-store dump (NTDS/SAM) followed by WinRAR password-protected staging
ucs.description: Hunts GALLIUM's documented post-DC-compromise tradecraft: dumping the NTDS.dit / SAM / SYSTEM hives via ntdsutil 'ifm', reg save, or VSS, then immediately archiving the dump (and other collected files) into a password-protected RAR for staged exfiltration. Microsoft (Granite Typhoon) and Cybereason (Soft Cell) both highlight rar.exe with the -hp flag and ntdsutil/reg save as a GALLIUM hallmark, distinct from groups that prefer 7-Zip or built-in PowerShell archive cmdlets.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as dumpTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where ((Processes.process_name="reg.exe" AND Processes.process="*save*" AND (Processes.process="*HKLM\\SAM*" OR Processes.process="*HKLM\\SECURITY*" OR Processes.process="*HKLM\\SYSTEM*")) OR (Processes.process_name="ntdsutil.exe" AND Processes.process="*ifm*") OR (Processes.process_name="vssadmin.exe" AND Processes.process="*create*" AND Processes.process="*shadow*") OR (Processes.process="*esentutl*" AND Processes.process="*ntds.dit*")) by host Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename process as dumpCmd, process_name as dumpTool | join type=inner host [ | tstats summariesonly=t min(_time) as archiveTime values(Processes.process) as archiveCmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("rar.exe","winrar.exe","rar32.exe") OR Processes.original_file_name IN ("Rar.exe","WinRAR.exe")) AND (Processes.process="* a *" OR Processes.process="*-hp*" OR Processes.process="*-p*" OR Processes.process="*-r *") by host Processes.user | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`] | where archiveTime >= dumpTime AND archiveTime <= dumpTime + 21600 | table dumpTime archiveTime host user dumpTool dumpCmd archiveCmd
ucs.kql:
// GALLIUM / Granite Typhoon — credential-store dump (ntdsutil/reg save/VSS) followed by
// password-protected RAR archive within 6h on the same host.
let Lookback = 7d;
let StageWindow = 6h;
let CredDump =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(Lookback)
    | where (FileName =~ "reg.exe"
             and ProcessCommandLine has "save"
             and ProcessCommandLine has_any (@"HKLM\SAM", @"HKLM\SECURITY", @"HKLM\SYSTEM", "hklm\\sam", "hklm\\security", "hklm\\system"))
        or (FileName =~ "ntdsutil.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "ifm")
        or (FileName =~ "vssadmin.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "create" and ProcessCommandLine has "shadow")
        or (FileName =~ "esentutl.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "ntds.dit")
        or (FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
            and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("Invoke-NinjaCopy", "ntds.dit", "DSInternals"))
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | project DumpTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, DumpAccount = AccountName,
              DumpTool = FileName, DumpCmd = ProcessCommandLine;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(Lookback)
| where (FileName in~ ("rar.exe","winrar.exe","rar32.exe")
         or InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("rar.exe","winrar.exe","rar32.exe")
         or ProcessVersionInfoOriginalFileName in~ ("Rar.exe","WinRAR.exe"))
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (" a ", " -hp", " -p", " -r ", " -m", " -v")
| join kind=inner CredDump on DeviceId
| where Timestamp between (DumpTime .. DumpTime + StageWindow)
| project DumpTime, ArchiveTime = Timestamp,
          DelayMin = datetime_diff('minute', Timestamp, DumpTime),
          DeviceName, DumpAccount, ArchiveAccount = AccountName,
          DumpTool, DumpCmd,
          ArchiveImage = FolderPath, ArchiveCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          ArchiveSHA256 = SHA256
| order by DumpTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// GALLIUM / Granite Typhoon — credential-store dump followed by password-protected RAR archive
let Lookback = 7d;
let StageWindow = 6h;
let CredDump =
    SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(Lookback)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | where (NewProcessName endswith "\\reg.exe"
             and CommandLine has "save"
             and CommandLine has_any ("HKLM\\SAM", "HKLM\\SECURITY", "HKLM\\SYSTEM"))
        or (NewProcessName endswith "\\ntdsutil.exe" and CommandLine has "ifm")
        or (NewProcessName endswith "\\vssadmin.exe" and CommandLine has "create" and CommandLine has "shadow")
        or (NewProcessName endswith "\\esentutl.exe" and CommandLine has "ntds.dit")
    | project DumpTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, DumpAccount = Account,
              DumpTool = NewProcessName, DumpCmd = CommandLine;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(Lookback)
| where EventID == 4688
| where NewProcessName has_any ("\\rar.exe", "\\winrar.exe", "\\rar32.exe")
| where CommandLine has_any (" a ", " -hp", " -p", " -r ", " -m", " -v")
| join kind=inner CredDump on Computer
| where TimeGenerated between (DumpTime .. DumpTime + StageWindow)
| project DumpTime, ArchiveTime = TimeGenerated,
          DelayMin = datetime_diff('minute', TimeGenerated, DumpTime),
          Computer, DumpAccount, ArchiveAccount = Account,
          DumpTool, DumpCmd,
          ArchiveImage = NewProcessName, ArchiveCmd = CommandLine
| order by DumpTime desc
ucs.rationale: The two-stage chain — credential-store dump tool *immediately* followed by rar.exe with -hp/-p/-r on the same host — is specifically what Microsoft (Granite Typhoon) and Cybereason (Soft Cell) attribute to GALLIUM. ntdsutil 'ifm' and reg save of SAM/SECURITY/SYSTEM are GALLIUM's preferred dumping methods (they avoid Mimikatz on DCs to reduce AV hits) and they have repeatedly been observed using ra
